---
title: "Sheet 04 â€” solutions"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

# Sheet 04 â€” solutions

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
cel <- read.csv("data/cel_volden_wiseman.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
```

**Ex. 1.**
```{r}
cel %>%
  filter(congress == 110) %>%
  group_by(dem) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), srednia_les = mean(les))
```
**Ans.: Republicans (dem=0) â‰ˆ 0.448; Democrats (dem=1) â‰ˆ 1.47.**

**Ex. 2.**
```{r}
cel$nazwisko <- str_extract(cel$thomas_name, "^[^,]+") # from start, chars != comma
# base R: sub(",.*$", "", cel$thomas_name)
```

**Ex. 3.**
```{r}
sum(str_detect(cel$thomas_name[cel$congress == 110], "^M")) # 48
```
**Ans.: 48.**

**Ex. 4.**
```{r}
sum(cel$congress == 110 & cel$female == 1) # 78
```
**Ans.: 78.**

**Ex. 5.**
```{r}
cel %>%
  filter(congress == 110) %>%
  group_by(st_name) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), les = mean(les)) %>%
  filter(n >= 5) %>%
  arrange(desc(les)) %>%
  head(1)
```
**Ans.: MN (Minnesota), mean les â‰ˆ 2.46.**

**Ex. 6.**
```{r}
n_distinct(cel$st_name[cel$congress == 110]) # 55
```
**Ans.: 55 (50 states + DC/territories).**

**Ex. 7.**
```{r}
cel %>%
  filter(congress == 110) %>%
  group_by(st_name) %>%
  mutate(sr_stan = mean(les)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  summarise(ilu = sum(les > sr_stan))
```
**Ans.: 149.**

**Ex. 8.**
```{r}
cel %>%
  filter(congress == 110) %>%
  arrange(desc(les)) %>%
  slice(1) %>%
  select(thomas_name, les)
```
**Ans.: Rangel, Charles (les â‰ˆ 18.69).**

**Ex. 9.**
```{r}
cel$imie <- str_trim(str_extract(cel$thomas_name, "(?<=,).*")) # after comma
# simpler: str_trim(sub("^[^,]+,", "", cel$thomas_name))
```

**Ex. 10.**
```{r}
cel %>%
  filter(congress == 110) %>%
  group_by(female) %>%
  summarise(les = mean(les))
```
**Ans.: women 1.06 vs men 0.99 â†’ women slightly higher (small difference).**

**Ex. 11.** â†’ **`str_detect()`**.

**Ex. 12.** â†’ **`grep("^United", x)`** (`^` = start of string).

**Ex. 13.** â†’ **replaces all occurrences**.
